note: STUB corpus demonstrating config-only specialty onboarding (Seamless PRD WS2). Adding this file + a SpecialtyConfig in specialties.py enables cardiology with ZERO pipeline changes — the Seedmaker, hardness judge, and hard-only serving are specialty-agnostic and read this config. Expand the archetypes + items with a cardiologist before production use.

failure_domains.name: heart_failure_gdmt
failure_domains.why: Sequencing/titrating the four GDMT pillars with low BP, hyperkalemia, or worsening renal function — competing risks the model flattens.

failure_domains.name: arrhythmia_anticoagulation
failure_domains.why: AF stroke/bleeding trade-offs, DOAC dosing in renal impairment, and periprocedural bridging traps.

failure_domains.name: acs_antithrombotic
failure_domains.why: DAPT duration and de-escalation balancing ischemic vs. bleeding risk — low-consensus judgment.

hardness_rubric: Requires multi-step reasoning, not single-fact recall.
Involves a genuine ischemic-vs-bleeding or efficacy-vs-tolerability trade-off.
Sits in a known model-failure domain for cardiology.
Contains a plausible trap (the guideline-reflex answer is wrong for this patient).
Rewards guideline nuance or a recent change.

hard_case_archetypes.why_hard: Initiating/sequencing ARNI + beta-blocker + MRA + SGLT2i in HFrEF with borderline SBP, CKD, and K 5.1 — which pillar first, which to hold.
AF anticoagulation in CKD-4 with prior GI bleed — DOAC vs. warfarin, dose-reduction criteria, and the eGFR misconception.
DAPT duration after PCI in a high-bleeding-risk patient on an anticoagulant — de-escalation timing and agent choice.

items.prompt: A 68-year-old with HFrEF (LVEF 28%) on lisinopril and carvedilol has SBP 104, eGFR 34, and K+ 5.1. How do you advance guideline-directed medical therapy, and in what order do you add or switch agents?
items.ai_failure_mode: Recommends adding all four pillars at once or switching to sacubitril/valsartan without accounting for borderline BP, CKD, and potassium.
items.why_high_value: Sequencing GDMT under competing constraints is a low-consensus, high-stakes judgment models routinely oversimplify.

items.prompt: A 79-year-old with nonvalvular AF (CHA2DS2-VASc 5), CKD-4 (eGFR 24), weight 58 kg, and a prior GI bleed needs stroke prevention. What anticoagulation strategy and dose do you choose, and what is the common dosing pitfall?
items.ai_failure_mode: Uses eGFR as an apixaban dose-reduction criterion or defers anticoagulation entirely because of the bleed history.
items.why_high_value: Balancing stroke vs. bleeding with renal dose nuance is a frequent, safety-critical model error.
items.reference_basis: Apixaban labeling dose-reduction criteria; AF anticoagulation guidance.

items.prompt: A 72-year-old on apixaban for AF undergoes PCI with a drug-eluting stent for NSTEMI. How do you structure antithrombotic therapy and DAPT duration given the high bleeding risk?
items.ai_failure_mode: Defaults to 12 months of triple therapy without de-escalation, ignoring bleeding-risk-guided shortening.
items.why_high_value: Triple-therapy duration is a nuanced, guideline-updated, high-bleeding-risk trade-off.
items.reference_basis: ACC/AHA ACS and DAPT guidance for patients on oral anticoagulation.
